$schema_notes.purpose: Canonical spec for the WEST TOWERS + FACADE of Notre-Dame de Paris — tier v2. Built on the same Yingzao pipeline as the spire (tier v1) and Nanchan: every dimensional/material/ornament node carries {provenance, source, url, rights}; nothing renders without a rights-cleared source; undocumented = GAP/conjecture, never invented. Prior tiers (Nanchan, the spire) are UNAFFECTED — this is a separate building key 'notre-dame-towers'.
$schema_notes.data_source: Every value traces to artifacts/towers-research-findings.json (adversarial cite-or-gap research, >=2 independent sources for 'measured', 68 datums: 43 measured / 11 reconstructed_design / 4 conjecture / 10 GAP). Public-domain drawings (Dehio-Bezold 1887, Viollet-le-Duc facade plates) are the asset sources; modern photos / paywalled papers are cite-only.

provenance_classes.measured: Multiply-confirmed published dimension/count (>=2 independent sources). Facts, uncopyrightable.
provenance_classes.reconstructed_design: From the 19th-c Viollet-le-Duc/Lassus restoration or PD drawings (Dehio-Bezold) — incl. the restored Gallery of Kings figures and the entirely-19th-c chimeras; or a single-source published figure.
provenance_classes.rule_derived: Derived by OUR engine from a Gothic geometry rule (ad quadratum/triangulum, two-centred arch, rose radial geometry, Roriczer pinnacle). Must cite the rule.
provenance_classes.conjecture: Flagged uncertain: contested estimate, exact colour, or fine modeling. Renders in conjecture colour; never presented as measured.
provenance_classes.GAP: Could not source. NEVER filled with a number.

units.module: pied du roi = 324.8 mm (= 0.3248 m); the cai-fen analog. Component positions/dimensions emitted in METRES so the verifier reads 43.5 m / 69 m / 9.6 m directly.

material_classes._note: The material-hypothesis toggle (ND spec point 4): as-built vs today are BOTH cited conjecture for exact colour (exact RGB is a documented GAP). The stone TYPE (Lutetian limestone) is measured; only its precise colour is conjecture.

source_registry._note: Rights-cleared registry. G09 (verify) HARD-FAILS any component whose source is not here with a use/use-data verdict. cite-only sources may be referenced in prose but never back a rendered value.

VLD-FACADE.citation: Viollet-le-Duc, Dictionnaire raisonné de l'architecture française — west-facade plates/elevations.
VLD-FACADE.reason: Author d.1879 → PD. The 19th-c restoration design drawings — the asset for facade form/ornament. reconstructed_design when scaled.

DEHIO-BEZOLD.citation: Dehio & von Bezold, Die kirchliche Baukunst des Abendlandes (atlas, 1887) — NDP facade plans/sections.
DEHIO-BEZOLD.reason: PD Mark 1.0 (UB Heidelberg). The cleanest open PD source for facade plans/sections; scaled dims = reconstructed_design.

FRIENDS-WEST-FACADE.citation: Friends of Notre-Dame de Paris — West Facade page.
FRIENDS-WEST-FACADE.reason: Facade 43.5 m wide, 45 m high, 69 m with towers; rose 9.6 m. Numbers only.

TECHNO-SCIENCE.citation: techno-science.net — NDP exterior architectural elements (FR-Wikipedia mirror, independent stream).
TECHNO-SCIENCE.reason: Independent confirmation of 43.5 m / 45 m / 9.60 m. Numbers only.

BNF-FACADE-9POINTS.citation: BnF Passerelles — 'La façade de Notre-Dame de Paris en 9 points'.
BNF-FACADE-9POINTS.reason: Institutional (BnF) confirmation of 43.5 m / 45 m / 69 m / rose 9.60 m / 28 kings / registers. Numbers/counts only.

EN-WIKI-NDP.reason: Cross-check for facade/towers facts. Numbers only.

PANORAMA-GALERIE-ROIS.citation: Panorama de l'art — Galerie des rois de la cathédrale Notre-Dame.
PANORAMA-GALERIE-ROIS.reason: 28 kings of Judah, ~3.5 m, the band; 19th-c restoration. Facts only.

CLUNY-HEADS.citation: Musée de Cluny — Heads of the Kings of Judah.
CLUNY-HEADS.reason: 21 original heads rediscovered 1977, held at Cluny; originals decapitated 1793. Facts only.

FRIENDS-ROSE-WINDOWS.citation: Friends of Notre-Dame de Paris — Rose Windows.
FRIENDS-ROSE-WINDOWS.reason: West rose 9.6 m, c.1225, radial tracery. Facts only.

FRIENDS-PORTALS.citation: Friends of Notre-Dame de Paris — Portals.
FRIENDS-PORTALS.reason: 3 portals: Virgin (L), Last Judgment (centre, widest), St-Anne (R). Facts only.

FR-WIKI-CHIMERES.citation: Wikipédia — Chimères de Notre-Dame de Paris.
FR-WIKI-CHIMERES.reason: Galerie des chimères = 19th-c Viollet-le-Duc/Lassus invention; Le Stryge; ~54 figures. Facts only.

FRIENDS-GARGOYLES.citation: Friends of Notre-Dame de Paris — Gargoyles.
FRIENDS-GARGOYLES.reason: Functional waterspouts vs decorative chimeras; largely 19th-c replacements. Facts only.

APOLLO-VLD.citation: Apollo Magazine — Viollet-le-Duc and the Gothic Revival restoration of Notre-Dame.
APOLLO-VLD.reason: Context: the chimeras/gargoyles seen today are VLD additions. Facts/context.

EN-WIKI-LUTETIAN.reason: The building stone is Lutetian limestone (Paris-basin Eocene). Facts only.

CNRS-MATERIALS.citation: CNRS News — Unveiling the secrets of Notre-Dame's materials.
CNRS-MATERIALS.reason: Stone/lead/oak material facts. Facts only (article prose cite-only).

EN-WIKI-SPIRE.citation: Wikipedia — Spire of Notre-Dame de Paris.

ARTNEWSPAPER-OAK-ROOF.citation: The Art Newspaper — green wood, axes and oaks (NDP oak framing).

CULTURE-GOUV-EMMANUEL.citation: Ministère de la Culture — Le bourdon Emmanuel.
CULTURE-GOUV-EMMANUEL.reason: Emmanuel bourdon ~13.3 t, south tower. Facts only.

FRIENDS-BELLS.citation: Friends of Notre-Dame de Paris — Bells.
FRIENDS-BELLS.reason: Bell programme (Emmanuel S tower; 8 bells N tower 2013). Facts only.

AD-QUADRATUM.citation: Ad quadratum: successive square side ratio 1/√2 (Salama et al. 2016; pure Euclid).
AD-QUADRATUM.reason: Pure geometry rule for facade proportion + pinnacle insets.

AD-TRIANGULUM.citation: Ad triangulum: triangle height = base × √3/2 (Hiscock; pure geometry).
AD-TRIANGULUM.reason: Pure geometry; elevation from width where no measured value exists (never overrides a sourced value).

TWO-CENTRED-ARCH.reason: Pure geometry for the portal arches (struck from springing points).

ROSE-RADIAL.reason: Radial spokes/tracery from the centre — pure geometry; the 24-ray count is from the cited tracery datum.

rights_excluded._note: Restricted / cite-only — never back a rendered component. Carried from the spire tier + the towers research's cite-only sources.

TALLON-SCAN.reason: Tallon laser cloud — no open license.

BORK-JSAH.reason: Bork JSAH 81(1) facade design-geometry — paywalled; cite the analysis, never ingest its derived numbers as measured.

NDP-OFFICIAL-FACADE-FR.reason: Official page prose/images — cite, don't ingest images.

MODERN-PHOTOS.reason: Any modern reference photograph of the facade/ornament is cite-only — never ingested as a texture/asset; procedural textures only.

meta.name_fr: Façade occidentale et tours de Notre-Dame de Paris
meta.name_en: West facade & towers of Notre-Dame de Paris
meta.build_state: The 'harmonic facade' (c.1200–1250), restored by Viollet-le-Duc & Lassus (1844–64); cleaned 1990s; post-2019-fire restoration completed 2024. The two towers were never given their planned spires.
meta.scope_note: WEST TOWERS + FACADE only. The spire (v1) and Nanchan are separate building keys and untouched.

total_width.note: VERIFIER ANCHOR. 43.5 m wide, multiply confirmed (3 independent streams).

body_height_no_towers.note: Facade body below the towers, ~141 pieds. Precise terminating register has a minor source spread (flagged in research).

register_scheme.value: bottom→top: (1) three portals; (2) Gallery of Kings (28 statues); (3) rose-window zone with the Gallery of the Virgin terrace + geminated bays; (4) Gallery of Chimères colonnade linking the towers; (5) the two square belfry towers
register_scheme.note: The harmonic-facade register sequence, corroborated across 3 streams.

buttress_count.note: Four vertical buttresses divide the facade into the tripartite bays.

_GAP_height_to_top_of_gallery_of_kings.note: DO NOT FILL. No clean published metric height to the TOP of the Gallery of Kings band (distinct from 43.5 m width / 45 m body). Must be scaled off the Dehio-Bezold PD section (rule_derived) or stay conjecture. A single unattributed '~20 m above ground' for the band exists (see gallery_of_kings.band_height_m, single-source → reconstructed_design).

height.note: VERIFIER ANCHOR. Both towers culminate at 69 m.

north_south_differ.value: yes — the north tower is slightly wider than the south (visible asymmetry)

north_tower_width.note: CITED-UNCERTAIN single source → reconstructed_design (not measured per the >=2-source rule).

spires_never_built.value: the two west towers were never given their intended spires (flat-topped belfries)

belfry_oak_framing.value: solid-oak bell yokes/belfry framing in the towers

emmanuel_bourdon.note: The great bourdon Emmanuel (~13.3 t) in the south tower.

north_tower_bell_set.value: 8 bells installed in the north tower in 2013

figure_count.note: VERIFIER ANCHOR. 28 figures across the facade.

identity.value: Kings of Judah (and Israel) — the royal ancestry of the Virgin/Christ; NOT the kings of France (a myth that led to their 1793 destruction)

band_height_m.note: CITED-UNCERTAIN (~20 m above ground, single/weak source) → reconstructed_design; used to place the band, not asserted as surveyed.

current_figures_origin.value: the figures on the facade today are Viollet-le-Duc's 19th-c restorations (Geoffroi-Dechaume workshop); 21 original heads, decapitated 1793, were rediscovered 1977 and are at the Musée de Cluny
current_figures_origin.note: So the rendered figures are reconstructed_design (19th-c), not medieval fabric.

diameter.note: VERIFIER ANCHOR. West rose 9.6 m (9.60 m), multiply confirmed; corpus high-end 9.7 m is one source's spread.

tracery.value: radial wheel tracery: ~24 radial spokes/rays in concentric rings
tracery.note: Radial geometry is rule_derived from the 24-ray count (cited).

centre_last_judgment.value: Portal of the Last Judgment (centre, the widest)

_GAP_portal_widths_m.note: DO NOT FILL. No published per-portal width in metres found (multiple sources confirm the centre is widest, but give no number). Bay widths are rule_derived by dividing the 43.5 m facade across the tripartite buttressed bays; never asserted as surveyed.

chimeras.note: ~54 chimères on the Galerie des Chimères — count is conjecture (varies by source). The chimeras are ENTIRELY 19th-c Viollet-le-Duc/Lassus inventions (NOT medieval) → render reconstructed_design (form) at conjecture count; Le Stryge is the famous one.

chimeras_are_19c.value: the Galerie des Chimères is a wholesale 19th-c Viollet-le-Duc/Lassus creation, not medieval fabric

gargoyles_19c_replacements.value: the gargoyles (functional waterspouts) seen today are largely 19th-c replacements; medieval ones eroded/removed

chimera_form_source.value: fine chimera/gargoyle modeling must come from the public-domain Viollet-le-Duc drawings; exact form beyond that is conjecture

_GAP_gargoyle_count.note: DO NOT FILL. No reliable total gargoyle count published (estimates vary wildly). Rendered gargoyles are a representative few flagged conjecture, never a sourced count.

_GAP_pinnacle_dims.note: DO NOT FILL. Tower buttress pinnacle dimensions not published; set out by the Roriczer rule (rule_derived), proportions conjecture.

_GAP_chimera_gallery_height.note: DO NOT FILL. Exact height of the Galerie des Chimères above ground not cleanly published.

verifier_targets._note: Recomputed by verify from component coordinates (never key_dimensions). T-prefixed for the towers tier. Metric tolerances.

checks.assert: Facade total width = 43.5 m (extreme facade component span in X)
Tower height = 69 m (top of the two tower components); facade body ≈ 45 m
Gallery of Kings = exactly 28 king figures across the facade band
Three west portals present (left Virgin, centre Last Judgment widest, right St-Anne)
West rose window diameter ≈ 9.6 m, radial tracery present
Four facade buttresses dividing the tripartite bays
Provenance audit: every component classed + sourced to the rights-cleared registry (use/use-data); 0 unsourced; all four classes present; every component {provenance,source,url,rights}
No-invention guard: no measured component cites a restricted/cite-only/single source; chimeras are reconstructed_design (19th-c), never measured; GAP-class never rendered with a number

Materials sourced: every facade/tower surface carries a material whose canonical entry resolves to a use/use-data source (Lutetian limestone measured; lead; oak); exact colour is conjecture not measured
Provenance view shows all four evidence-class colours

ideal_deviations_index._note: Where the real facade disagrees with a generic ideal — kept, never corrected.
items: Facade 43.5 m / towers 69 m / rose 9.6 m are MEASURED — never recomputed from an ad-quadratum/triangulum ideal (T02 critical).
North and south towers DIFFER in width (asymmetric) — not normalized to identical twins.
The towers were never given spires — rendered flat-topped, not 'completed' to a Gothic ideal.
Gallery-of-Kings figures + the chimeras are 19th-c restoration/invention (reconstructed_design), never presented as medieval fabric.
Exact stone colour (as-built vs cleaned vs sooted) is conjecture; exact RGB is a GAP — never invented.
